title: Blood Pressure Measurement in Mice Using Tail Cuff Method
author: Jennifer Rutkowsky
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yetften
materials: Allow mouse to sit on the warming pad until a body temp of 32-35ºC is achieved. Use the infrared thermometer at the base of the tail to read the subject’s body temperature.
A window labeled “Basic Session Info” will appear.
Type in your name under the technician section.
Change gloves between groups of mice.

steps.title: Place mouse into an appropriate sized restrainer
steps.instruction: Place mouse into an appropriate sized restrainer.

steps.title: Place the tail through the groove...
steps.instruction: Place the tail through the groove of the back of the restrainer and tighten screw.

steps.title: Feed the tail through the occlusion...
steps.instruction: Feed the tail through the occlusion cuff first and then through the VPR cuff second. Ensure a snug fit but do not force the tail through either cuff as this will cause constriction and yield false pressure readings.

steps.title: Make sure the body temperature does not exceed 39ºC
steps.instruction: Make sure the body temperature does not exceed 39ºC.

steps.title: Click on the CODA icon on the supplied laptop
steps.instruction: Click on the CODA icon on the supplied laptop.

steps.title: A window will appear with a line that reads
steps.instruction: A window will appear with a line that reads: “CODA 8 – Channels 1-8 (8)” in red letters. Highlight this line and click on “Use these devices.”

steps.title: Click on File>New>Experiment and a “Begin...
steps.instruction: Click on File>New>Experiment and a “Begin New Experiment” window will appear.

steps.title: Type in the name of the experiment and click next
steps.instruction: Type in the name of the experiment and click next.

steps.title: Enter the session name and set the following parameters
steps.instruction: Enter the session name and set the following parameters: - Number of Sets: 1 - Time Between Sets: 00:00:30 - Cycles per Set: 20 - Time Between Cycles: 00:00:05

steps.title: Click next and a “Specimen Selection” window will appear
steps.instruction: Click next and a “Specimen Selection” window will appear.

steps.title: Click “Manage Specimens” and enter the animal IDs
steps.instruction: Click “Manage Specimens” and enter the animal IDs. Use the + button to add additional mice.

steps.title: Save this information and close the window when completed
steps.instruction: Save this information and close the window when completed.

steps.title: Under the “Specimen Pool” highlight each...
steps.instruction: Under the “Specimen Pool” highlight each animal and click the right arrow to assign them to a channel on the right side of the screen.

steps.title: Click next and a window labeled “Session Parameters” will appear
steps.instruction: Click next and a window labeled “Session Parameters” will appear.

steps.title: Set the following session parameters
steps.instruction: Set the following session parameters: - Max Occlusion Pressure: 250 - Deflation Time: 20 - Min Volume: 15

steps.title: Under the “Display Style” function, choose one channel per graph
steps.instruction: Under the “Display Style” function, choose one channel per graph.

steps.title: Click next and a window labeled “Session Script” will appear
steps.instruction: Click next and a window labeled “Session Script” will appear.

steps.title: Make sure the occlusion wait time is set at
steps.instruction: Make sure the occlusion wait time is set at

steps.title: Ensure all cuffs are properly secured...
steps.instruction: Ensure all cuffs are properly secured and that the mice have reached a temperature of 32-35ºC.

steps.title: Click next, then finish to start the experiment
steps.instruction: Click next, then finish to start the experiment.

steps.title: Once the experiment is over, remove...
steps.instruction: Once the experiment is over, remove mice and wipe off each restraint 10% Nolvasan before adding more mice.

steps.title: Once all experiments have completed, use...
steps.instruction: Once all experiments have completed, use 10% Nolvasan to clean equipment (do not use alcohol) and wipe the surrounding bench top with Coverage Plus working solution (diluted to 1/256 in water).
